zh.Northampton, Northamptonshire, England: 英國諾薩普頓史爾諾薩普頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Hubert La Veyle於1118年出生在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾諾薩普頓時，他的父親Eustace La Veyle29歲，母親Mrs. Eustace la Veyle28歲。他和Mrs. Hubert la Veyle至少有1個兒子。他去世在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.Northampton, Northamptonshire, England: 英国諾薩普頓史爾諾薩普頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hubert La Veyle于1118年出生在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾諾薩普頓时，他的父亲Eustace La Veyle29岁，母亲Mrs. Eustace la Veyle28岁。他和Mrs. Hubert la Veyle至少有1个儿子。他去世在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: La
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Veyle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Devon and Cornwall) and Irish (Waterford): variant of Veal . This is the usual form of the surname in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as de Bhial.

variants.name: Veal
meanings: nickname from Anglo-Norman French viel, Old French vieil ‘old’ (i.e. ‘the elder’). Without the article, this name is indistinguishable from the relationship name Vial , and there has also been some confusion with Vail and Vale .
nickname for a docile, calf-like person, from Middle English vel(e), veille, fel(e) ‘calf; veal’, Old French veel ‘calf’.

nameMeanings.name: Hubert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. It was popular among the Normans, who introduced it to Britain, where it was later reinforced by settlers from the Low Countries. An 8th-century St Hubert succeeded St Lambert as bishop of Maastricht and is regarded as the patron of hunters, since, like St Eustace , he is supposed to have seen a vision of Christ crucified between the antlers of a stag.

variants.name: Hupprecht
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. It was popular among the Normans, who introduced it to Britain, where it was later reinforced by settlers from the Low Countries. An 8th-century St Hubert succeeded St Lambert as bishop of Maastricht and is regarded as the patron of hunters, since, like St Eustace , he is supposed to have seen a vision of Christ crucified between the antlers of a stag.

variants.name: Huppert
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. It was popular among the Normans, who introduced it to Britain, where it was later reinforced by settlers from the Low Countries. An 8th-century St Hubert succeeded St Lambert as bishop of Maastricht and is regarded as the patron of hunters, since, like St Eustace , he is supposed to have seen a vision of Christ crucified between the antlers of a stag.

variants.name: Hugbert
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. It was popular among the Normans, who introduced it to Britain, where it was later reinforced by settlers from the Low Countries. An 8th-century St Hubert succeeded St Lambert as bishop of Maastricht and is regarded as the patron of hunters, since, like St Eustace , he is supposed to have seen a vision of Christ crucified between the antlers of a stag.

en.lifeSummary: When Hubert La Veyle was born in 1118, in Northampton, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Eustace La Veyle, was 29 and his mother, Mrs. Eustace la Veyle, was 28. He had at least 1 son with Mrs. Hubert la Veyle. He died in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hubert La Veyle est né en 1118, à Northampton, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Eustace La Veyle, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Eustace la Veyle, avait 28 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Mrs. Hubert la Veyle. Il est décédé dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hubert La Veyle 1118 in Northampton, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Eustace La Veyle 29 und seine Mutter Mrs. Eustace la Veyle 28. Er hatte mit Mrs. Hubert la Veyle mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hubert La Veyle è nato il nel 1118 nel Northampton, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Eustace La Veyle, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Mrs. Eustace la Veyle, aveva 28 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Mrs. Hubert la Veyle. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.Northampton, Northamptonshire, England,: イギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアノーサンプトン
ja.Northampton, Northamptonshire, England: イギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアノーサンプトン
ja.lifeSummary: Hubert La Veyleが1118年にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアノーサンプトンで生まれた時，父Eustace La Veyleは29歳，母Mrs. Eustace la Veyleは28歳であった。彼はMrs. Hubert la Veyleとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Hubert La Veyle(이)가 1118년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 노르섬프턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Eustace La Veyle(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Eustace la Veyle(은)는 28세였다. 그는 Mrs. Hubert la Veyle(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

mn.Northampton, Northamptonshire, England,: Northampton, North Hamtunscire, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hubert La Veyle nasceu em 1118, em Northampton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Eustace La Veyle, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Eustace la Veyle, tinha 28 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Mrs. Hubert la Veyle. Ele faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hubert La Veyle родился в 1118 г. в местности нортамптон, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Eustace La Veyle было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Eustace la Veyle, 28. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Mrs. Hubert la Veyle. Он умер в его родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hubert La Veyle nació en 1118, en Northampton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Eustace La Veyle, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Mrs. Eustace la Veyle, tenía 28 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Mrs. Hubert la Veyle. Murió en su pueblo.
